title: NATO Edge Act
plain_summary: This bill makes it much harder for the President to pull the United States out of NATO without getting permission from Congress first. The bill also makes sure that NATO ally countries spend enough money on their own defense like they promised to do. If a President tries to leave NATO without following these new rules, Congress can take them to court to stop it. These new rules will stay in place until 2033.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on Rules, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the NATO Edge Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Findings
sections.text: Congress believes the following facts. The North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO) has been a foundation for peace around the world, an important part of United States security, and a way to stop enemies and outside dangers. The House of Representatives stated in a resolution on June twenty seventh, two thousand seventeen that NATO is one of the most successful military partnerships in history. NATO has prevented another world war, protected member countries from invasion, and helped end the Cold War peacefully. NATO remains the foundation of United States foreign policy to promote wealth, freedom, and peace in Europe. The United States is deeply committed to NATO's rule of helping defend each other as written in Article Five of the North Atlantic Treaty. Congress condemns any threat to the independence, borders, freedom, and democracy of any NATO ally. Congress strongly supports working with NATO allies to protect Ukraine's borders against unprovoked attacks from Russia. NATO members should follow the decision made at the NATO Wales Summit in two thousand fourteen. That decision said each alliance member should try to spend at least two percent of their country's total economic output on defense by two thousand twenty four. Investing in training exercises with multiple countries and joint research and education programs with NATO allies have made improvements. These improvements help countries work together better and be more ready against threats to European security, especially from Russia.

sections.title: Section 3 Modification of Limitation on Withdrawal from NATO
sections.text: Section twelve fifty A of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year two thousand twenty four is changed in the following ways. In part a, after the words an Act of Congress, add this language. The President can only withdraw if all remaining NATO members who have not spent at least two percent of their total economic output on defense have made a clear promise to spend two percent within five years. Part b is completely rewritten to say this. No money approved by any law can be used to support any decision by any United States government official to stop, end, reject, or withdraw the United States from the North Atlantic Treaty. This treaty was signed in Washington DC on April fourth, nineteen forty nine. Withdrawal can only happen with the advice and agreement of the Senate, provided that two thirds of the Senators present agree, or through an Act of Congress. Also, all remaining NATO members who have not spent at least two percent of their total economic output on defense must make a clear promise to spend two percent within five years. The existing parts c, d, e, and f are renumbered as parts f, g, h, and i. After part b, three new parts are added. Part c says that by adopting a resolution, the Senate or House of Representatives can authorize their legal counsel to start or join court cases. These cases would be in federal court to oppose any action to withdraw from NATO that does not follow this section. Part d says that any resolution about withdrawing from NATO must be considered using specific procedures from the International Security Assistance and Arms Export Control Act of nineteen seventy six. Part e says that any legal counsel working under section twelve ninety nine R must report quickly to the House Foreign Affairs Committee and the Senate Foreign Relations Committee about any court cases they start or join.

sections.title: Section 4 Sunset
sections.text: The changes made by section three of this law will end on September thirtieth, two thousand thirty three. On that date, Section twelve fifty A of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year two thousand twenty four will go back to how it was before these changes were made.
